sections.paragraph:
σύνδεσμος, ου, ὁ (συνδέω; Eur., Thu. et al.; LXX; Jos., Ant. 3, 120) and τὸ σύνδεσμον B 3:3 (the pl. σύνδεσμα is found occasionally beside σύνδεσμοι) prim. ‘that which binds together’.

① that which holds someth. together, fastener lit. (Appian, Bell. Civ. 4, 115 §483 οἱ σύνδεσμοι of the fastenings that hold the various ships together; Herm. Wr. 1, 18; EpArist 85=‘fastening’) ligaments of the body (Eur., Hipp. 199 al.; on Galen’s usage s. Lghtf., Col p. 199; Straub 33f) w. ἁφή Col 2:19 pl.

② that which brings various entities into a unified relationship, uniting bond fig. ext. of 1; σύνδ. τῆς εἰρήνης the bond of peace, i.e. that consists in peace (epexegetic gen.; Plut., Numa 63 [6, 4] σύνδεσμος εὐνοίας κ. φιλίας; W-S. §30, 9b) Eph 4:3. Love is σύνδεσμος τῆς τελειότητος the bond that unites all the virtues (which otherwise have no unity) in perfect harmony or the bond of perfect unity for the church Col 3:14 (cp. Simplicius In Epict. p. 89, 15 Düb. οἱ Πυθαγόρειοι … τὴν φιλίαν … σύνδεσμον πασῶν τ. ἀρετῶν ἔλεγον; Pla., Polit. 310a.—S. also Pla., Leg. 21, 5, 921c of law: τῆς πόλεως ς.).—On σύνδεσμος as a philos. concept: WJaeger, Nemesios v. Emesa 1918, 96–137. KReinhardt, Kosmos u. Sympathie 1926; AFridrichsen, Serta Rudbergiana ’31, 26, SymbOsl 19, ’39, 41–45; GRudberg, ConNeot 3, ’39, 19–21.

③ a bond that confines/hinders, fetter only fig. σύνδεσμος ἀδικίας (Is 58:6) fetter that consists in unrighteousness Ac 8:23 (s. also 4 below); B 3:3, 5 (in the two last-named passages Is 58:6 and 9 are quoted in context).

④ entities that are held together by a bond, bundle (of cattle food O. Mich 240, 2 [III–IV a.d.]; 239, 2 [IV a.d.]); in imagery Ac 8:23 according to Mft., Goodsp. et al. (s. 3 above). By fig. ext. band, college (Herodian 4, 12, 6 ὁ ς. τῶν ἐπιστολῶν) ς. ἀποστόλων ITr 3:1.—DELG s.v. 1 δέω. M-M. TW. Sv.

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

Thu Thu , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

  1. pl. = plural

prim. prim. = primarily

someth. someth. = something

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Galen Galen , II a.d.—List 5

Lghtf. Lghtf. = JBLightfoot

Straub Straub = WS., Die Bildersprache des Apostels Paulus—List 6

ext. ext. = extension

i.e.

i.e. = id est (that is)

I.-E. Indo-European

gen. gen. = genitive

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

W-S. W-S. = GWiner, Grammatik des neutestamentlichen Sprachidioms, ed. PSchmiedel—List 6

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Simplicius Simplicius , VI a.d.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

SymbOsl SymbOsl = Symbolae Osloenses—List 6

ConNeot ConNeot = Coniectanea Neotestamentica—List 6

Mft. Mft. = JMoffatt, The Bible, A New Translation—List 6

Goodsp. Goodsp. = EGoodspeed; with no title specified, this abbr. refers to The New Testament: An American Translation—List 6

Herodian Herodian = Herodianus Historicus, III a.d.—List 5

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Sv Sv (at the end of entries)=HSieben, Voces—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 966
